title: GetResponse vs Substack (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: GetResponse vs Substack: compare pricing, features, and real use cases to find the right email tool for your needs.
intro: Choosing between GetResponse and Substack comes down to your specific use case. Both are legitimate email marketing platforms, but they serve different audiences. GetResponse is built for online course creators and webinar hosts, while Substack targets writers monetising directly from subscribers. This comparison covers the key differences so you can decide quickly.

comparison_table.tool_a: Online Course Creators And Webinar Hosts

choose_a_if: You are primarily focused on online course creators and webinar hosts
choose_b_if: You are primarily focused on writers monetising directly from subscribers

faq.a: It depends on your use case. GetResponse wins for online course creators and webinar hosts, while Substack is stronger for writers monetising directly from subscribers. Neither is objectively better — the right choice depends on your specific needs.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, GetResponse or Substack?
faq.a: Substack starts at a lower price point ($0/mo vs $19/mo). However, pricing scales with subscriber count, so the total cost depends on your list size.

faq.q: Can I switch from Substack to GetResponse?
faq.a: Yes, switching from Substack to GetResponse is possible. Most platforms support CSV import/export for subscriber lists. Plan for some time to recreate automations and templates, as these rarely transfer directly between platforms.
